name: Texas-Style Bagels - Ak
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir yeast into 1 1/2 cups warm water in a large bowl. Let stand until bubbly, about 5 minutes.
2. Stir in sugar, salt, pepper, Tabasco sauce, and 3 tablespoons of jalapeno pepper. Gradually add enough flour to form a soft dough.
3. Knead the dough on a lightly floured board until smooth and elastic, adding more flour as needed.
4. Let the dough stand in a warm place for 15 minutes. Punch down the dough.
5. Roll the dough into a rectangle measuring about 13 x 4 inches and 1 inch thick on a lightly floured board.
6. Cut the dough into 18 strips with a floured knife.
7. Roll each strip gently to form a rope that is 1/2 inch thick.
8. Moisten the ends of each rope and press together to form bagels.
9. Place the bagels on floured cookie sheets. Let stand covered for 20 minutes.
10. Heat one gallon of water to boiling in a large stockpot. Reduce heat to medium. Place 3 to 4 bagels in the pot. Simmer uncovered for 7 minutes (do not simmer longer).
11. Drain on a towel. Repeat with the remaining bagels.
12. Preheat oven to 375 DEGREES.
13. Brush the bagels with beaten egg and sprinkle with the remaining jalapeno pepper.
14. Bake the bagels on an ungreased cookie sheet for 30-35 minutes, or until golden brown.
15. Cool on a wire rack. Serve with cold sliced roast beef or chicken.
